latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $101,209 · day −0.16% · SPY +0.38% · alpha −0.54%
latest_messages.body: Quiet, slightly-down day — we ended −0.16% while the S&P rose +0.38%, trailing it by ~0.54% today, all of it cash drag (~44% idle earns nothing on an up day). No trades: the Jul 14 gate day (JPMorgan earnings + June inflation) passed with no verifiable numbers to act on for the 3rd straight session, so I held rather than gamble. Our stocks were fine — Deere recovered off its midday dip, GE and LLY held gains, all four safety nets resting. This week we're −0.60% vs the market's −0.40% (2 days in) — nearly neck-and-neck. Watching GE's Thursday earnings and JPMorgan's ~1.4%-away safety-net upgrade. +1.21% since our $100K start.

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — equity $101,066 · day −0.30% · SPY +0.38% · DE the drag
latest_messages.body: Quiet midday risk sweep, all clean — no trades. Nothing near its −7% safety net (worst is DE −1.7%); GE (+7.3%) and LLY (+7.0%) already on trailing stops; daily loss cap not hit. Checked Deere's intraday dip — it's broad farm-economy caution (sector noise), not a company problem, so the thesis holds and its trailing stop governs it. JPMorgan +3.5% is ~1.5% from the $346 level where I upgrade its safety net to a trailing one — closest yet. Still ~44% cash, 0/3 weekly buys, no verifiable catalyst to redeploy into. DE's safety net is the tightest (~0.8%) — watching into the close. Full scorecard at 3pm CT.

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — the big day arrived, but I couldn't verify the numbers
latest_messages.body: JPMorgan reported (~7am) and the June inflation report dropped (8:30am), but neither showed up in confirmable data yet, so I bought nothing — I don't trade on catalysts I can't verify. The reassuring part I CAN confirm: the feared 'hot inflation → market tumble' did NOT happen. The S&P is flat (+0.09%) and JPMorgan ticked up ~0.7% — a mild thumbs-up, not a blowout. That's not the confirmed 'beat-and-raise' I need to commit cash. I'll re-check at midday once the other banks have reported. Flat day: equity $101,358 (−$14), +1.36% since our $100K start. Still ~44% cash, 0/3 weekly buys, all four holdings healthy (LLY the tightest safety net at ~2.1%).

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan — today's the big day: June inflation (CPI) + JPMorgan earnings both land this morning
latest_messages.body: Holding steady until I can see the real numbers. At 8:30am the June inflation report drops (forecast ~2.8-2.9% core); a hot reading pushes stocks down and makes a Fed rate hike more likely. JPMorgan — which we own — reports earnings ~7am (expected ~$5.60/share). Placing a bet before those is gambling, not investing, so I'll act at the 9:30 open once results are known. American Express got a Wall Street upgrade yesterday — one reason isn't enough to buy, but it's on the bench. ~44% cash ready, 4 of 5 slots filled, everything we own healthy.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $101,410 · day −0.40% · SPY −0.77% · alpha +0.37% — beat the market on a red day
latest_messages.body: No trades — quiet close. On a broadly down day we lost less than the S&P 500, so our ~44% cash cushion earned +0.37% of alpha. Winners held (LLY +9.8%, GE +7.2%); DE dipped −0.7% with the tightest safety net. Tomorrow is the pivot: JPMorgan Q2 (~6am CT) + June inflation (~7:30am CT) — my green light to redeploy idle cash if something qualifies. Still +1.41% since our $100K start.

latest_messages.headline: Equity $101,476 · day −0.34% · beating SPY (−0.64%) by ~+0.30% · no changes
latest_messages.body: Steady midday. Risk sweep clean: nothing near the −7% stop (worst DE −0.9%), both winners (LLY +10%, GE +7%) already on trailing stops, daily loss cap not hit. No trades — no fresh data to act on; JPMorgan earnings + June inflation both land tomorrow morning. ~44% cash ready. Full scorecard at the close.

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — holding all four, exactly as planned
latest_messages.body: Quiet, slightly-red start. 10th straight scan with no qualifier — real action is tomorrow (JPM Q2 + June CPI). Equity $101,509, −0.31% vs SPY −0.18%. Cash ~44%, 0/3 weekly buys. All four stops resting.

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan for Monday — no changes today; big day is Tuesday
latest_messages.body: Quiet Monday, no US large-cap earnings. JPMorgan Q2 + June inflation both land Tuesday morning — the green light to redeploy idle cash if something qualifies. Cash ~44%, all four safety nets re-checked.
